title: [CI:DOCS] Fix up buildah images
body:
Fedora no longer ships /etc/containers/storage.conf but
/usr/share/containers/storage.conf, this causes the buildimages
to fail to build. This PR uses the storage.conf in /usr.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Thx for running with this, was just getting ready to fix up 4033.  I don't see a CentOS7 Containerfile and you've deleted the Dockerfile, was that intentional?

@cevich, PTAL
Yes I don't see a reason to ship a centos7 version.
One last one, did you update the "stablebyhand" one too?  Sorry for the late comment, I didn't think of it until late last night.

> One last one, did you update the "stablebyhand" one too?

My PR nukes that one.  No reason to have it when the normal images build fine "by hand".  #4019 will begin daily builds which are monitored (as opposed to github-actions builds).

> Yes I don't see a reason to ship a centos7 version.

This is the kind of stuff that _needs_ to be in the commit log message.
Note: I am not touching ./contrib/buildahimage/stablebyhand/ since I am not sure what this is for.  It is badly out of date.  Is anyone using it?

> Is anyone using it?

I'm pretty sure it's (very) legacy and can be safely removed.  I removed it from podman and skopeo a long while ago and nobody complained :grin:
